title: Angolluma dummeri
intro: Angolluma dummeri, also known as Dummer's Milk Pudica, is a succulent plant species native to the island of Socotra in Yemen. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its reported health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Angolluma dummeri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Angolluma dummeri is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried powder for use in treating a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Angolluma dummeri is classified in the plant family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the larger order Gentianales, which includes several other plant families. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group, consisting of over 5,000 species across 300 genera. Angolluma dummeri is a unique species within this family, native to the deserts of the Arabian Peninsula. Further classification of Angolluma dummeri at the genus and species level is based on its distinctive characteristics and physical features.
